top_signals.title: Nuclear Watchdog Eyes Stronger Reactor Defenses After UAE Attack
top_signals.summary: Iran’s attack last month on a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates has prompted the world’s atomic watchdog to look at whether reactors’ external power supply lines need additional protection against the threat of war.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: XRP falls toward $1.10 as liquidation-driven selloff pushes token to multi-month lows
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Fannie, Freddie Whipsaw as Trump Floats Trillion-Dollar Value
top_signals.summary: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac common shares rose sharply at the open on Friday after President Donald Trump said the two mortgage finance giants were probably worth $1 trillion, though they subsequently erased much of those gains as skepticism mounted.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: SpaceX’s $75 Billion IPO Draws More Orders than Shares Available
top_signals.summary: SpaceX has already received orders for more than the shares available in its record-setting $75 billion initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, shortly after starting to formally market the deal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: US Prosecutors are Scrutinizing Marks in Private Credit, Clayton Says
top_signals.summary: ends around 18:20 A discussion with US Attorney and former SEC Chairman Jay Clayton (Interviewed by Sridhar Natarajan) (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Congo’s traditional healers are on the front line of Ebola fight
top_signals.summary: In a region where health workers are shunned, an effective response to Ebola relies on integrating communities and "their rites," the World Health Organization says.

top_signals.title: Man arrested over ¥15 million December burglary in western Tokyo
top_signals.summary: The man admitted to allegations of being involved in burglary in December, telling investigators he recruited the offenders and gave instructions.
